t1: This is not a new idea. The dystrophin gene (11,000 bp) was far too large for AAV. Researchers created "micro-dystrophin" by removing non-essential spectrin-like repeats, fitting it into a single AAV vector. Sarepta's SRP-9001 (delandistrogene moxeparvovec) using this approach was FDA-approved in 2023. Ultra-Mini STRC follows the same template with a tighter cassette budget: 2,103 bp CDS vs micro-dystrophin's ~3,600 bp, leaving more room for OHC-specific regulatory elements.
